title: Make Errors and Status Recoverable
summary: Build and verify accessible errors status in failed survey submission recovery incident through prediction, inspection, explanation, cumulative code, and changed-case transfer.
objectives: Explain accessible errors status from purpose, behavior, and evidence.
Apply accessible errors status without discarding earlier valid work.
Diagnose the named misconception in changed content, input, and failure states.

starterFiles.html: <!-- Continue and harden the Community Needs Survey artifact here. -->

steps.title: 01 · Predict the failed survey submission recovery incident case · theory
steps.instruction: Checkpoint 1: Which claim preserves accessible errors status when data, input, and layout change? Scenario: failed survey submission recovery incident.
steps.why: Make Errors and Status Recoverable makes the decision observable and testable. This is checkpoint 1 in failed survey submission recovery incident.

content.title: failed survey submission recovery incident evidence
content.text: Choose the claim that preserves purpose, native behavior, and understandable relationships when presentation changes.

hints: Make Errors and Status Recoverable checkpoint 1: identify the user task and data relationship first.
Make Errors and Status Recoverable checkpoint 1: compare source, native behavior, and announced meaning.
Make Errors and Status Recoverable checkpoint 1: make the smallest repair that preserves earlier passing work.

options.text: Failed survey submission recovery incident case 1: color and a red border give every learner enough error information.

Failed survey submission recovery incident case 1: summarize what failed, connect specific text to each invalid field, move focus deliberately, preserve input, and announce status at the right urgency.
Failed survey submission recovery incident case 1: clear the form after failure so users can enter cleaner data on the next attempt.

options.text: failed survey submission recovery incident phase 4.1: trigger blank, malformed, boundary, server, and repeated-submit failures
failed survey submission recovery incident phase 4.2: confirm visible text, programmatic association, preserved input, and focus route
failed survey submission recovery incident phase 4.3: verify announcement timing and remove stale status before the next attempt
failed survey submission recovery incident phase 4.4: correct one value, resubmit, and confirm both error removal and success status

steps.title: 05 · Read the failed survey submission recovery incident case · theory
steps.instruction: Checkpoint 5: Which claim preserves accessible errors status when data, input, and layout change? Scenario: failed survey submission recovery incident.
steps.why: Make Errors and Status Recoverable makes the decision observable and testable. This is checkpoint 5 in failed survey submission recovery incident.

content.text: Errors need specific text, programmatic connection to affected controls, preserved user input, and a predictable focus route. Color and borders may supplement but cannot carry the message alone. Dynamic status belongs in an appropriate live region; urgent blocking errors may use alert behavior, while routine progress should remain polite. Repeated submission must not create duplicate or stale announcements. Apply it to failed survey submission recovery incident.
